name: 张家界
description: Zhangjiajie, 'The Home of Avatar', is famous for its UNESCO-listed national forest park featuring towering sandstone pillars, dramatic karst formations, and lush forests. The city's unique pillar-like quartz sandstone pillars inspired the floating mountains in the movie Avatar. Beyond the park, visitors explore Tianmen Mountain's glass skywalk, the world's longest glass bridge, ancient Tujia villages, and mystical caves.
highlights: Zhangjiajie National Forest Park - UNESCO World Heritage
Tianmen Mountain - Glass skywalk and sky cave

climate.tips: Mountain weather is unpredictable - bring layers. Summer monsoon season brings heavy rain. Winter can see snow on peaks. Morning mists create magical scenery. Book accommodation in park area for early access to attractions.

attractions.name: 张家界国家森林公园
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage site featuring thousands of sandstone pillars, dense forests, and dramatic gorges. The Avatar Hallelujah Mountains inspiration.
attractions.tips: Stay inside park to avoid re-entry fees. Wake early for sunrise at viewing platforms. The park is huge - plan routes carefully.

attractions.name: 袁家界
attractions.description: Famous for the Avatar Hallelujah Mountains - towering sandstone pillars that appear to float in mist.
attractions.tips: Take the world's highest elevator (326m) to Yuanjiajie. Most crowded in morning.

attractions.name: 天门山
attractions.description: Known for the Heavenly Door cave and world's longest glass skywalk. The 99 bends road leading up is legendary.
attractions.tips: Book online to avoid queues. Go early. Glass Skywalk requires separate ticket. The summit can be foggy.

attractions.name: 张家界大峡谷玻璃桥
attractions.description: The world's longest and highest glass-bottomed bridge, 430m long and 300m above the canyon.
attractions.tips: Book at least one day ahead. Don't wear high heels. Maximum 800 visitors per day.

attractions.name: 黄龙洞
attractions.description: One of China's largest caves with stalactites, stalagmites, underground rivers and the world's tallest stalagmite.
attractions.tips: Audio guide highly recommended. Boat ride included. Temperature inside is cooler year-round.

attractions.name: 宝峰湖
attractions.description: A scenic lake known as 'Mini Lijiang' with traditional Tujia village on its shores.
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby Huanglong Cave. Evening performances in summer.

attractions.name: 土家风情园
attractions.description: A theme park showcasing traditional Tujia architecture, customs, and performing arts.
attractions.tips: Best visited in afternoon to catch performances. Combine with Tianmen Mountain.

attractions.name: 金鹰卡通
attractions.description: Iconic TV tower in city center, symbol of Zhangjiajie with rotating restaurant at top.
attractions.tips: Best visited at sunset for day/night views. Restaurant requires reservation.

attractions.name: 天门山玻璃栈道
attractions.description: A terrifying 60m glass walkway along cliffs with drop views, world's highest bungee jump nearby.
attractions.tips: Not for those afraid of heights. Separate queue for skywalk. Bungee jump tickets sold separately.

attractions.name: 索溪峪
attractions.description: A nature reserve with diverse flora and fauna, known for macaque monkeys.
attractions.tips: Don't feed the monkeys. Keep belongings secure. They can be aggressive.

attractions.name: 杨家界
attractions.description: Quieter area with Emperor Mountain, Four Screening Walls, and fewer crowds than Yuanjiajie.
attractions.tips: Best for escaping crowds. Take shuttle bus to Yangjiajie. Good photography spot.

attractions.name: 墨子湾
attractions.description: Scenic area featuring lotus-shaped mountain and traditional fishing village.
attractions.tips: Best at sunset. Combine with day trips. Free entry but parking fee.

attractions.name: 十里画廊
attractions.description: A scenic train ride through towering pillars resembling famous paintings and sculptures.
attractions.tips: Train saves walking but misses some scenery. Get off at viewpoint for photos.

attractions.name: 黄石寨
attractions.description: A plateau area with panoramic views, known as 'The Largest Natural Amphitheater in The World'.
attractions.tips: Take cable car up, walk down. Early morning best for views. Famous filming location.

attractions.name: 金鞭溪
attractions.description: A beautiful 7.5km stream famous for golden sand and crystal clear water.
attractions.tips: Best visited in morning. Flat terrain easy to walk. Bring water and comfortable shoes.

attractions.name: 天子山
attractions.description: The 'King of the Peaks' with stunning views and the标志性御笔峰.
attractions.tips: Cable car recommended. Best lighting in late afternoon. Cloud seas appear in early morning.

attractions.name: 马蜂窝
attractions.description: A scenic area with traditional Tujia stilted buildings and local crafts.
attractions.tips: Good for understanding Tujia architecture. Buy local specialties here.

attractions.name: 张家界大瀑布
attractions.description: A spectacular 300m waterfall inside the forest park, one of the highest in China.
attractions.tips: Get soaked approaching the falls. Bring raincoat or umbrella.

attractions.name: 永定古镇
attractions.description: A 2000-year-old Tujia town with preserved ancient streets and traditional houses.
attractions.tips: Best at night when illuminated. Try local snacks. Connected to Tujia culture.

attractions.name: 玉皇顶
attractions.description: The highest peak in Zhangjiajie at 1216m with panoramic views of the pillar landscape.
attractions.tips: Take cable car to summit. Best visibility in morning. Wear warm layers.

attractions.name: 百龙电梯
attractions.description: The world's highest outdoor elevator at 326m, transporting visitors from ground level to Yuanjiajie in just 66 seconds.
attractions.tips: Long queues in peak season. Consider walking up if you have time. Combined ticket with park is cheaper.

attractions.name: 黄龙洞
attractions.description: Combined ticket for Yellow Dragon Cave and Baofeng Lake, offering the best value for visiting both attractions.
attractions.tips: Purchase combined ticket to save money. Visit cave in morning, lake in afternoon. Both are must-see attractions.

attractions.name: 蒙泉湖
attractions.description: A pristine mountain lake known for its emerald waters and surrounding peaks. Popular for bamboo rafting.
attractions.tips: Best visited in spring and autumn. Bamboo rafting is the main attraction. Bring change of clothes for water activities.

attractions.name: 茅岩河
attractions.description: A scenic river for white water rafting and kayaking, flowing through dramatic limestone gorges.
attractions.tips: Bring waterproof bags for valuables. Wear quick-dry clothes. Safety equipment provided. Not suitable for non-swimmers.

attractions.name: 仙人桥
attractions.description: A natural stone arch bridge formed by erosion, spanning a dramatic gorge. One of Zhangjiajie's most-photographed spots.
attractions.tips: Best photographed in morning light. Can be slippery when wet. Combine with other Yangjiajie attractions.

attractions.name: 土家秀
attractions.description: Evening performance showcasing Tujia folk songs, dances, and the famous 'Xiji Girl' love story.
attractions.tips: Book through hotel concierge for discounts. Bring jacket as outdoor venue. Shows run rain or shine.

attractions.name: 新黄龙洞
attractions.description: Expanded cave system with improved lighting and walkways, offering extended exploration routes.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds. Audio guide recommended. Wear comfortable walking shoes. Temperature inside is cool.

attractions.name: 武隆
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage site featuring massive sinkholes, bridges, and caves. Famous filming location for movie Transformers.
attractions.tips: Requires day trip from Zhangjiajie. Best combined with Fengdu or Wulong. Book tour for convenience. Photo ops are stunning.

attractions.name: 武隆芙蓉洞
attractions.description: One of the largest developed caverns in China with spectacular stalactite and stalagmite formations.
attractions.tips: Part of Wulong day trip. Combine with Three Natural Bridges. Bring warm clothes for cave temperature.

attractions.name: 天生三桥
attractions.description: Three natural limestone bridges - Dragon Bridge, Qinglong Bridge, and Heilong Bridge - forming an impressive natural architecture.
attractions.tips: Take shuttle bus from entrance. Walk downhill through the bridges. Elevators available as alternative. Stunning scenery.

attractions.name: 张家界大峡谷玻璃桥
attractions.description: 世界最长最高的玻璃桥，长430米，高300米。
attractions.tips: 体验高空玻璃桥。

attractions.name: 天门山
attractions.description: 有天门洞和玻璃栈道，是张家界最著名的景点之一。
attractions.tips: 体验高空玻璃栈道。

attractions.name: 张家界国家森林公园
attractions.description: 世界地质公园，有独特的砂岩峰林地貌。
attractions.tips: 电影《阿凡达》取景地。

attractions.name: 天桥
attractions.description: 张家界天然石拱桥。
attractions.tips: 了解地质奇观。

attractions.name: 张家界美食
attractions.description: 张家界有土家族特色美食。
attractions.tips: 品尝张家界美食。

attractions.name: 张家界博物馆
attractions.description: 展示张家界历史和地质的博物馆。
attractions.tips: 了解张家界文化。

attractions.name: 天子山
attractions.description: 张家界最高的山峰，可俯瞰整个景区。
attractions.tips: 看云海日出。

attractions.name: 金鞭溪
attractions.description: 张家界最美的溪流，全长7.5公里。
attractions.tips: 漫步溪边。

attractions.name: 宝峰湖
attractions.description: 张家界的人工湖，有高峡平湖之称。
attractions.tips: 乘船游览。

attractions.name: 黄龙洞
attractions.description: 中国最长的溶洞之一，有定海神针等景观。
attractions.tips: 了解溶洞奇观。

attractions.name: 溪布街
attractions.description: 张家界最有特色的文化街区。
attractions.tips: 体验土家族文化。

attractions.name: 杨家界
attractions.description: 张家界新开发的景区，有天然长城之称。
attractions.tips: 人少安静。

attractions.name: 袁家界
attractions.description: 张家界最著名的景区，有乾坤柱等景观。
attractions.tips: 电影《阿凡达》取景地。

attractions.name: 天下第一桥
attractions.description: 天然石拱桥，高350米，跨度50米。
attractions.tips: 了解地质奇观。

attractions.name: 大庸古城
attractions.description: 张家界新开发的历史文化街区。
attractions.tips: 了解张家界历史。

attractions.name: 天门洞
attractions.description: 天门山上的天然穿山溶洞，高131米。
attractions.tips: 攀登999级台阶。

attractions.name: 老道湾
attractions.description: 张家界新开发的民俗文化村。
attractions.tips: 了解土家族文化。

attractions.name: 张家界夜景
attractions.description: 溪布街的夜景灯光秀。
attractions.tips: 夏季夜间最美。

attractions.name: 普光禅寺
attractions.description: 张家界最古老的佛教寺院。
attractions.tips: 了解宗教文化。

attractions.name: 三下锅
attractions.description: 张家界最著名的特色菜。
attractions.tips: 品尝张家界特色菜。

restaurants.name: 三下锅总店
restaurants.description: Famous for San Xia Guo - a traditional Tujia hot pot with three meats.
dishHighlights: San Xia Guo (Three Meats Hot Pot)

restaurants.name: 岩耳炖鸡
restaurants.description: Specialty restaurant serving Rock Ear Chicken - a famous local medicinal soup.

restaurants.name: 老灶台三下锅
restaurants.description: Popular local restaurant with authentic old stove cooking method.

restaurants.name: 土家扣肉馆
restaurants.description: Specializing in Tujia-style braised pork and traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: 葛根粉店
restaurants.description: Local snack shop serving authentic kudzu root powder desserts.

restaurants.name: 猕猴桃汁
restaurants.description: Fresh kiwi juice from Zhangjiajie's famous kiwi fruit.

restaurants.name: 腊肉豆腐馆
restaurants.description: Specializing in smoked pork and tofu dishes.

restaurants.name: 特色野菜馆
restaurants.description: Serving fresh wild vegetables from the forest park area.

restaurants.name: 土家风味楼
restaurants.description: Upscale Tujia restaurant with traditional decor and performances.

restaurants.name: 路边野菜摊
restaurants.description: Roadside stalls selling freshly picked mountain vegetables.

restaurants.name: 竹筒饭馆
restaurants.description: Serving rice cooked in bamboo tubes - a local specialty.

restaurants.name: 农家乐
restaurants.description: Rural homestyle cooking with fresh ingredients from local farms.

restaurants.name: 苗家酸汤鱼
restaurants.description: Miao-style sour soup fish - a specialty of the region.

restaurants.name: 豆腐脑店
restaurants.description: Popular breakfast spot serving tofu brain with various toppings.

restaurants.name: 湘菜馆
restaurants.description: Classic Hunan restaurant with spicy dishes and rice.

restaurants.name: 大排档
restaurants.description: Night market with variety of grilled meats and skewers.

restaurants.name: 米粉店
restaurants.description: Local rice noodles with savory broth and toppings.

restaurants.name: 蒸菜馆
restaurants.description: Traditional Tujia steamed dishes - healthy and flavorful.

restaurants.name: 火锅城
restaurants.description: Large hot pot restaurant with variety of broths and ingredients.

restaurants.name: 土家腊肉
restaurants.description: Specializing in traditional Tujia smoked and cured meats.

restaurants.name: 张家界川菜馆
restaurants.description: Popular Sichuan restaurant with signature mapo tofu and spicy dishes.

restaurants.name: 武陵土菜馆
restaurants.description: Local favorite serving authentic Tujia dishes with home-cooked style.

restaurants.name: 山顶农家菜
restaurants.description: Cozy restaurant inside the park with stunning mountain views and farm-fresh ingredients.

restaurants.name: 溪水鱼庄
restaurants.description: Specializing in stream fish dishes - fresh trout and carp from local waters.

restaurants.name: 回民餐厅
restaurants.description: Halal restaurant serving beef and lamb dishes in Uyghur and Chinese Muslim style.

restaurants.name: 天门山索道餐厅

restaurants.name: 张家界火锅店
restaurants.description: Popular hot pot restaurant with variety of broths and fresh ingredients.

restaurants.name: 五雷山素斋
restaurants.description: Temple vegetarian cuisine with organic vegetables and Buddhist-style dishes.

restaurants.name: 夜市烧烤一条街
restaurants.description: Famous BBQ street with dozens of stalls serving grilled meats and skewers.

restaurants.name: 张家界国际酒店餐厅
restaurants.description: Hotel restaurant offering international buffet and Chinese dishes.

arrival.from: Major domestic airports: Beijing (3.5h), Shanghai (2.5h), Guangzhou (1.5h)
arrival.tips: Airport 5km from city center. Airport bus (20¥) to city and scenic areas. Taxis available.

arrival.to: Zhangjiajie Railway Station / Zhangjiajie West Station
arrival.tips: High-speed trains from Changsha. West Station for high-speed, main station for regular trains.
Bus station near train station. Tourist buses to scenic areas available.

metro.description: Connects city center to airport and bus station
Connects railway stations to main tourist areas
Lines to Wulingyuan and scenic areas planned
Limited English signage - use translation app
Connects to bus stations for scenic area access
When metro crowded, buses are good alternative
Get city map at metro stations

bus.description: Within Forest Park - free with ticket

taxi.description: Agree price before for tourist areas

bike.description: Bikes not allowed inside most attractions

hotels.name: 张家界禾田居度假酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best luxury option near scenic areas. Book directly for free upgrades.

hotels.name: 张家界京武铂尔曼酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best Western brand in city. Weekend discounts available.

hotels.name: 张家界万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best new luxury hotel. Book river-view rooms.

hotels.name: 张家界铂尔曼酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for park visitors. Shuttle to park entrance included.

hotels.name: 张家界天房酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best mid-range option in city center. Request quiet rooms.

hotels.name: 张家界国宾酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good for business travelers. Weekend rates lower.

hotels.name: 张家界福朋喜来登酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for those with cars. Free parking available.

hotels.name: 张家界锦江之星
hotels.bookingTips: Best budget chain option. Book online for discounts.

hotels.name: 张家界如家酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Most reliable budget chain. Ask for renovated rooms.

hotels.name: 张家界7天连锁酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for one-night stays. Book ahead in peak season.

hotels.name: 张家界客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Best for park visitors. Owner can arrange tickets.

hotels.name: 张家界山上客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Book well ahead - limited rooms. Bring warm clothes.

hotels.name: 张家界民宿
hotels.bookingTips: Authentic local experience. Minimum 1 night stay.

hotels.name: 张家界天门山酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for Tianmen visitors. Early start to mountain.

hotels.name: 张家界客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Good balance of price and quality. Ask about packages.

hotels.name: 张家界温德姆花园酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good value for money. Quiet area away from main road.

hotels.name: 张家界华天大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Reliable mid-range option. Book direct for better rates.

hotels.name: 武陵源御笔峰酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best location for park visitors. Shuttle service available.

hotels.name: 张家界锦江都城酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for train travelers. Quick access to station.

hotels.name: 张家界东玉温泉酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Perfect after touring. Combine with cave visit.

hotels.name: 张家界在路上客栈
hotels.bookingTips: Popular with independent travelers. Great for solo travelers.

payment.description: Most widely accepted mobile payment in Zhangjiajie
tips: Use for most purchases in city

payment.description: Essential for Didi ride-hailing and many local shops
Essential in Zhangjiajie - many local vendors and scenic areas only accept cash
howToUse: Bamboo raft operators usually cash only

payment.description: Accepted at hotels and larger restaurants
Chinese bank cards - useful for ATM withdrawals

howToUse: Apple Pay/Google Pay at participating stores
tips: Still need cash for rural areas

culturalTips.title: Tujia Culture
culturalTips.content: Zhangjiajie is home to the Tujia people, an ethnic minority with unique customs. Respect their traditions when visiting villages. The 'Nuo opera' is a traditional performance art worth witnessing.

culturalTips.title: Miao Influences
culturalTips.content: Nearby areas have Miao ethnic communities. Sample sour soup fish and other Miao dishes. The silver jewelry and embroidery are traditional crafts.

culturalTips.title: Morning Mists
culturalTips.content: Zhangjiajie's pillars often appear floating in mist - this is the classic Avatar scenery. Early morning (before 8am) offers the best views with least crowds.

culturalTips.title: Weather in Mountains
culturalTips.content: Mountain weather changes rapidly - sunny morning can turn foggy by noon. Bring layers and rain gear. Temperature inside park can be 5-10C cooler than city.

culturalTips.title: Glass Attractions
culturalTips.content: Zhangjiajie has multiple glass attractions - the Glass Bridge and Glass Skywalk. Those afraid of heights should avoid or view from safe distances. Photo opportunities are plentiful.

culturalTips.title: Bungee Jump
culturalTips.content: The world's highest bungee jump (326m) is at the Glass Bridge area. Extreme thrill for adventurous visitors. Medical clearance recommended.

culturalTips.title: San Xia Guo
culturalTips.content: The signature dish 'Three Meats Hot Pot' (San Xia Guo) is a must-try. Pork, lamb, and chicken cooked in a single pot with vegetables. Find authentic versions in Wulingyuan.

culturalTips.title: Wild Vegetables
culturalTips.content: Mountain vegetables (野菜) are a specialty - freshly picked from the forest. Try ferns, bamboo shoots, and mushrooms. Health-conscious and flavorful.

culturalTips.title: Kudzu Root Products
culturalTips.content: Kudzu root (葛根粉) is a local specialty - used in soups and desserts. Believed to have health benefits. Available at night markets.

culturalTips.title: Kiwi Fruit
culturalTips.content: Zhangjiajie is famous for kiwi fruit - sweet and cheap in season (September-October). Fresh juice stalls are everywhere. Don't miss trying them.

culturalTips.title: Tipping Culture
culturalTips.content: Tipping is not customary in Zhangjiajie. At hotels and restaurants, service charge is rarely added. Small tips for exceptional service are appreciated but not expected.

culturalTips.title: Photography
culturalTips.content: The scenery is highly photogenic. For sunrise at viewing platforms, arrive 30 minutes early to secure position. Tripods allowed but be mindful of other visitors.

culturalTips.title: Park Transportation
culturalTips.content: The park has efficient shuttle bus system included in ticket. The Bailong Elevator (Hundred Dragon Elevator) is the world's highest outdoor elevator - saves time but often long queues.

culturalTips.title: Walking in Park
culturalTips.content: The park is huge - 500km2. Don't try to see everything in one day. Prioritize Yuanjiajie for Avatar mountains, Golden Whip Stream for easy walking, and Yangjiajie for fewer crowds.

culturalTips.title: Environmental Protection
culturalTips.content: Zhangjiajie is a UNESCO site - please don't litter. The park has strict environmental rules. Stay on marked paths to protect the unique ecosystem.

culturalTips.title: Wildlife
culturalTips.content: The forest park has macaque monkeys. Don't feed them or leave food visible - they can be aggressive and will take food from bags. Keep distance.

culturalTips.title: Local Products
culturalTips.content: Look for Tujia silver, handmade embroidery, and dried wild herbs. For authentic products, visit the morning market or Tujia villages rather than tourist shops.

culturalTips.title: Tea
culturalTips.content: Zhangjiajie has local tea including Wuling Mountain Tea. Buy tea at specialty shops or from tea plantations. Bargaining expected at markets.

culturalTips.title: Local Alcohol
culturalTips.content: Tujia people have a strong brewing tradition. Try the local millet wine (粟米酒) and Tujia fermented grain wine (土家咂酒). Rice wine is also popular. Drinking together from the same jar is a traditional bonding ritual.

culturalTips.title: Wild Mushroom Season
culturalTips.content: Summer and autumn bring an abundance of wild mushrooms. Zhangjiajie's forests produce prized varieties like matsutake and chanterelles. Mountain restaurants often serve fresh-picked mushrooms in season.

culturalTips.title: Tujia Greetings
culturalTips.content: The Tujia say 'ni hao' like other Chinese, but have unique customs. When receiving guests, Tujia families may offer tea with both hands. It is polite to accept a small sip before setting the cup down.

culturalTips.title: Photography Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Some Tujia villages and sacred sites prohibit photography. Always ask permission before photographing locals, especially elderly people. At viewpoints, be courteous about sharing space for photos.

culturalTips.title: Altitude and Health
culturalTips.content: Some scenic areas are at 1000m+ altitude. If you have heart or breathing conditions, take it slow. The Bailong Elevator provides a quick ascent - some visitors experience ear popping. Stay hydrated.

culturalTips.title: Park Shuttle Bus
culturalTips.content: The park's shuttle bus system is efficient but can be crowded. Peak times are 10:00-14:00. Get on early at major stops. Buses run until 18:00 - plan your day to avoid being stranded.

culturalTips.title: Leave No Trace
culturalTips.content: As a UNESCO World Heritage site, Zhangjiajie has strict environmental protection. Pack out all trash, stay on marked trails, and never remove plants, rocks, or other natural objects. Penalties for violation can be steep.

culturalTips.title: Weather and Dress
culturalTips.content: Mountain weather changes rapidly. Even in summer, bring a light jacket and rain gear. Temperatures inside the park can be 5-10 degrees cooler than in the city. Wear comfortable, non-slip shoes for trails.

culturalTips.title: Antiques and Crafts
culturalTips.content: Be cautious when buying 'antiques' or 'ancient' items. Most are reproductions. For authentic crafts, buy directly from Tujia artisans in villages. Tujia silver and embroidery are genuine traditional crafts.

张家界旅游信息服务中心
emergencyContacts.notes: Tourist information, maps, and travel advice

新加坡驻广州总领事馆

quickFacts.visa: Most nationalities require visa. 144h transit visa available at airport.
quickFacts.water: Use bottled water - mountain springs safe but packaged water recommended
quickFacts.bestTimeToVisit: April-June, September-October for best weather and scenery
